content: If $$y=m_1 x+c_1$$ and $$y=m_2 x+c_2, m_1 \neq m_2$$ are two common tangents of circle $$x^2+y^2=2$$ and parabola $$y^2=x$$, then the value of $$8\left|m_1 m_2\right|$$ is equal to
explanation:
Given, $$y=m_1 x+c_1$$ is common tangent of circle and parabola.

For circle, $$\left|\frac{c_1}{\sqrt{1+m_1^2}}\right|=\sqrt{2} \Rightarrow c_1^2=2\left(1+m_1^2\right)$$ .... (i)

For parabola, $$c_1=\frac{1}{4 m_1} \Rightarrow c_1^2=\frac{1}{16 m_1^2}$$ .... (ii)

Also, $$y=m_2 x+c_2$$ is common tangent of circle and parabola.

For circle, $$\left|\frac{c_2}{\sqrt{1+m_2^2}}\right|=\sqrt{2} \Rightarrow c_2^2=2\left(1+m_2^2\right)$$

For parabola, $$c_2=\frac{1}{4 m_2} \Rightarrow c_2^2=\frac{1}{16 m_2^2}$$

Now, $$2\left(1+m_1^2\right)=\frac{1}{16 m_1^2} \quad$$ [From Eqs. (i) and (ii)]

$$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad 32 m_1^4+32 m_1^2-1=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad m_1^2=\frac{-32 \pm \sqrt{(32)^2-4(32)(-1)}}{2 \times 32} \end{aligned}$$

$$\Rightarrow \quad m_1^2=\frac{-32+24 \sqrt{2}}{64}$$

[Ignoring negative value because $$m^2 \geq 0$$]

$$\Rightarrow m_1= \pm \sqrt{\frac{-32+24 \sqrt{2}}{64}}$$

Similarly, $$m_2= \pm \sqrt{\frac{-32+24 \sqrt{2}}{64}}$$

$$\begin{aligned} \therefore 8\left|m_1 m_2\right| =8\left|\left( \pm \sqrt{\frac{-32+24 \sqrt{2}}{64}}\right)\left( \pm \sqrt{\frac{-32+24 \sqrt{2}}{64}}\right)\right| \\ =\frac{8}{64}(-32+24 \sqrt{2}) \\ =-4+3 \sqrt{2} \end{aligned}$$

content: If the straight line $$y = mx + c$$ touches the parabola $${y^2} - 4ax + 4{a^3} = 0$$, then c is
explanation:
Solving the given equations,

$${(mx + c)^2} = 4ax - 4{a^3}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {m^2}{x^2} + 2mc\,.\,x + {c^2} = 4ax - 4{a^3}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {m^2}{x^2} + (2mc - 4a)x + {c^2} + 4{a^3} = 0$$

Since the straight line touches the parabola at a point, so, the discriminant = 0

$$ \Rightarrow {(2mc - 4a)^2} - 4{m^2}({c^2} + 4{a^3}) = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow 4{m^2}{c^2} - 16amc + 16{a^2} - 4{m^2}{c^2} - 16{a^3}{m^2} = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow - mc + a - {a^2}{m^2} = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow mc = a - {a^2}{m^2} \Rightarrow c = {a \over m} - {a^2}m$$

content: A normal is drawn at the point P to the parabola $${y^2} = 8x$$, which is inclined at 60$$^\circ$$ with the straight line $$y = 8$$. Then the point P lies on the straight line
explanation:
For the parabola $${y^2} = 4ax$$, the equation of normal at $$P(a{m^2}, - 2am)$$ is $$y = mx - 2am - a{m^3}$$.

Here, $$m = \tan 60^\circ = \sqrt 3 $$

$$\therefore$$ $$P \equiv (a{(\sqrt 3 )^2}, - 2a(\sqrt 3 )) \equiv (6, - 4\sqrt 3 )$$ ($$\because$$ $$a = 2$$)

Thus, P satisfies $$2x - y - 12 - 4\sqrt 3 = 0$$

content: For each parabola y = x2 + px + q, meeting coordinate axes at 3-distinct points, if circles are drawn through these points, then the family of circles must pass through
explanation:
Suppose the parabola, y = x2 + px + q cuts X-axis at A($$\alpha$$, 0) and B($$\beta$$, 0).

Then, $$\alpha$$, $$\beta$$ are roots of the equation x2 + px + q = 0

$$\therefore$$ $$\alpha$$ + $$\beta$$ = $$-$$p and $$\alpha \beta$$ = q

The parabola y - x2 + px + q cuts Y-axis at (0, q).

Let the equation of the circle passing through A, B and C be

x2 + y2 + 2gx + 2fy + c = 0 .... (i)

$$\therefore$$ $$\alpha$$2 + 2g$$\alpha$$ + c = 0 ..... (ii)

$$\beta$$2 + 2g$$\beta$$ + c = 0 ...... (iii)

and q2 + 2fq + c = 0 ..... (iv)

Subtracting Eq. (iii) from Eq. (ii), we get

$$\alpha + \beta + 2g = 0$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ g = p/2

Adding Eqs. (ii) and (iii), we get

$$\alpha^2+\beta^2+2g(\alpha+\beta)+2c=0$$

$$(\alpha+\beta)^2-2\alpha \beta+2g(\alpha+\beta)+2c=0$$

p2 $$-$$ 2q $$-$$ p2 + 2c = 0 [$$\because$$ $$\alpha+\beta=p$$ and g = $$\frac{p}{2}$$]

Putting c = q in Eq. (iv), we get f = $$-$$$$\left(\frac{q+1}{2}\right)$$

Substituting the values of g, f and c in Eq. (i), we obtain the equation of family of circles passing through A, B and C as

x2 + y2 + px $$-$$ (q + 1) y + q = 0

Clearly, it passes through (0, 1).

content: The origin is shifted to (1, 2). The equation y2 $$-$$ 8x $$-$$ 4y + 12 = 0 changes to y2 = 4ax, then a is equal to
explanation:
Let P(x, y) be the original position of the point w.r.t. the original axes. Let us move the origin at new position to (h, k).

Hence, the position of the same point P in the new system is

x' = x $$-$$ h $$\Rightarrow$$ y' = y $$-$$ k

Here, (h, k) = (1, 2)

$$\therefore$$ x' = (x $$-$$ 1), y' = (y $$-$$ 2)

According to the question,

y2 $$-$$ 8x $$-$$ 4y + 12 = (y $$-$$ 2)2 $$-$$ 4a(z $$-$$ 1)

$$\Rightarrow$$ y2 $$-$$ 8x $$-$$ 4y + 12 = y2 $$-$$ 4y + 4 $$-$$ 4ax + 4a

On comparing respective coefficients, we get

4a = 8 $$\Rightarrow$$ a = 2

content: The distance of point of intersection of the tangents to the parabola x = 4y $$-$$ y2 drawn at the points where it is meet by Y-axis, from its focus is
explanation:
Given equation of parabola is

x = 4y $$-$$ y2

$$\Rightarrow$$ y2 $$-$$ 4y + 4 = $$-$$ x + 4

$$\Rightarrow$$ (y $$-$$ 2)2 = $$-$$ (x $$-$$ 4)

a = $${{1} \over 4}$$

Focus = $$\left( {{{15} \over 4},2} \right)$$

Parabola cuts Y-axis of (0, 0) and (0, 4)

Now, equation of tangent at (0, 0) is

x = 4y ...... (i)

and equation of tangent at (0, 4) is

x + 4y = 16 .... (ii)

Solving Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get

x = 8, y = 2

$$\therefore$$ Intersection point of tangent is P(8, 2).

Distance from point P to focus S

Distance = $$\sqrt {{{\left( {8 - {{15} \over 4}} \right)}^2} + {{(2 - 2)}^2}} $$

$$ = 8 - {{15} \over 4} = {{17} \over 4}$$
